109## Conventions an agent must respect102## Conventions an agent must respect
110103
n111The top rules for safely modifying this code. Full set in `docs/project/conventions.md`.n104The top rules for safely modifying this code:
112105
n113- **Component prop types live in ONE file** — `src/components.d.ts` holds every component's props interface. This is intn1061. **All prop types live in `src/components.d.ts`** — a single 648-line file with 70 exported `interface`/`type` declara
>entional; do **not** colocate per-component. See `docs/project/conventions.md` "Don't refactor".>tions. This is **intentional**, not technical debt. Add new prop interfaces there, not next to the component. (See "Don'
 >t refactor" below.)
114- **One file per component family**, not per component — e.g. `feedback.tsx` ships Alert, Modal, Drawer, Progress, Skele1072. **Re-export every new component from `src/index.tsx`.** Anything not re-exported is invisible to consumers — `src/ind
>ton, Spin, Tooltip, toast, ToastRegion together. Re-exported by family in `src/index.tsx`.>ex.tsx` is the contract.
115- **CSS class names use `.rcs-*` BEM-ish convention** — see `src/styles.css`. Variants encoded as `.rcs-button--primary`1083. **Style with `.rcs-*` class names defined in `src/styles.css`.** Don't introduce CSS-in-JS, CSS Modules, or Tailwind 
>, slots as `.rcs-modal-header`. Don't switch styling solutions.>— the project deliberately uses hand-rolled BEM. 251 classes today (`grep -oE '\.rcs-[a-z0-9-]+' src/styles.css | sort -
 >u | wc -l`).
116- **Tokens are CSS variables** in `colors_and_type.css` (root file, NOT inside `src/`). Theme swap via `[data-theme="lig1094. **Token *types* go in `src/tokens.ts`; token *values* go in `colors_and_type.css`.** Components consume types at comp
>ht|dark"]` on `<html>`. Don't hard-code hex except in the `BRAND_HEX` map in `src/components/theme.tsx`.>ile time and the CSS variables at runtime. Don't introduce a JS token object — there isn't one and there shouldn't be.
117- **Adding a new component** = create file in `src/components/`, add prop interface to `src/components.d.ts`, add styles1105. **Theme switching is `data-theme="light|dark"` on `<html>`.** Set by `ThemeProvider` (`src/components/theme.tsx:30–34
> to `src/styles.css` with `.rcs-*` names, add one re-export line to `src/index.tsx`. (See `README.md > Editing component>`). Don't introduce alternative theming mechanisms (context-based, prop-drilled).
>s`.) 
118- **Strict event signatures** — props use typed handlers (`(e: MouseEvent<HTMLButtonElement>) => void`), never `(value: 1116. **Strong typing is the product.** From `src/components.d.ts:1–14`: discriminated unions for variants, strict event si
>any) => void`. See `src/components.d.ts:39` and the file's header comment.>gnatures (never `(value: any) => void`), `readonly` on array props. New code must match.
119- **`readonly` on prop fields** including arrays — see the top-of-file comment in `src/components.d.ts`.1127. **Zero runtime deps.** Only `react` + `react-dom` as `peerDependencies` (`package.json:46–49`). Don't add a runtime d
 >ependency without explicit user approval.
120- **Zero runtime deps** — only React/React-DOM (peer). Do not introduce a runtime dependency without explicit approval.
121113
122## Gotchas114## Gotchas
123115
n124- **No tests exist yet** despite vitest being installed; CI's test step has `continue-on-error: true` (`.github/workflown116- **Build script concatenation, not bundling.** `pnpm build` in `package.json:37` runs `tsc` *and* a Node one-liner that
>s/ci.yml:40`). Don't trust a green CI as evidence of correctness; manual verification via `preview/*.html` cards is the > does `writeFileSync('dist/styles.css', readFileSync('colors_and_type.css') + '\n' + readFileSync('src/styles.css'))`. I
>current QA mechanism.>f you rename either CSS file or move `colors_and_type.css` out of the project root, the build silently produces a broken
 > stylesheet. Update the script in lockstep.
125- **No eslint config** yet; CI lint step also `continue-on-error` (`.github/workflows/ci.yml:36`). Adding an eslint conf117- **`tsconfig.build.json` excludes `src/styles.css` explicitly** (`tsconfig.build.json:8`). The build relies on this — T
>ig will start failing CI unless those `continue-on-error` flags are removed in the same change.>ypeScript would otherwise try to type-check the CSS file via the broad `include` glob.
126- **Build script is a one-liner with embedded Node `-e`** (`package.json:38`) that reads `colors_and_type.css` (root) + 118- **No ESLint config in the repo.** `package.json:39` declares `lint: eslint ...` and CI calls it, but no `.eslintrc*` /
>`src/styles.css` and writes `dist/styles.css`. If you rename either CSS file, this breaks silently — `tsc` would still s> `eslint.config.*` exists (verify via `find . -maxdepth 2 -name '.eslintrc*' -o -name 'eslint.config.*'`). CI sets `cont
>ucceed but `dist/styles.css` would either error or be incomplete.>inue-on-error: true` for the lint step (`.github/workflows/ci.yml:34`). Adding a config will turn lint failures into har
 >d CI failures — coordinate the change.
127- **`tsconfig.build.json` excludes `src/styles.css`** (`tsconfig.build.json:7`) — TypeScript wouldn't include it anyway,119- **No tests yet.** Vitest is wired but zero `*.test.*` / `*.spec.*` files exist (verify via `find . -name '*.test.*' -n
> but the explicit exclude is a load-bearing reminder that styles ship via the build script's CSS-concat step, not via ts>ot -path '*/node_modules/*'`). CI also soft-fails the test step (`continue-on-error: true` at `.github/workflows/ci.yml:
>c.>38`). When you add the first test, plan to remove that flag.
128- **Release workflow refuses to publish on tag/version mismatch** — `.github/workflows/release.yml` "Verify tag matches 120- **No Storybook or visual testing.** Component QA today is manual via the 38 HTML cards in `preview/` and the device-fr
>package version" step exits 1 if `git tag` (`vX.Y.Z`) != `package.json:version`. Use `pnpm version <patch|minor|major>` >ame viewer at `preview/responsive-check.html`. Those cards are **static HTML** referencing the CSS — they do *not* impor
>which bumps both atomically.>t the React components. Visual changes must be checked manually in both `preview/` and the live components.
129- **Dual-registry publish** — same package goes to npm (`registry.npmjs.org`) AND GitHub Packages (`npm.pkg.github.com`)121- **Release workflow refuses to publish if `package.json` version != git tag.** `.github/workflows/release.yml` reads ve
> under the same `@ossrandom` scope. The GPR job rewrites `publishConfig.registry` in-place via inline Node before `npm p>rsion, compares with `${GITHUB_REF_NAME#refs/tags/}`, and `exit 1`s on mismatch. Use `pnpm version <bump>` (which writes
>ublish`. Bumping or changing `publishConfig` in `package.json` may collide.> both atomically) instead of editing `package.json` by hand.
130- **Toast requires consumer to mount `<ToastRegion />`** somewhere in the tree, then call `toast(...)` imperatively. For122- **`SKILL.md` at repo root is content shipped to package consumers**, not a project-summary doc. It's a Claude skill ("
>getting the region means `toast()` is silently a no-op. Source: `src/components/feedback.tsx`, exported in `src/index.ts>randomcodespace-design") that downstream prototypes invoke. Don't read it as authoritative project metadata.
>x`. 
131- **`useTheme` outside `ThemeProvider` returns a no-op fallback** that reads `prefers-color-scheme` and ignores `setMode123- **`ui_kits/*/README.md` and the `preview/` HTML cards are reference *output*, not the library's source.** They contain
>`/`toggle` calls (`src/components/theme.tsx:65`). Bugs reported as "theme toggle does nothing" usually mean missing prov> inline React + Babel and use the CSS tokens directly. Editing them does not affect what the package publishes.
>ider — not a bug in the toggle. 
132- **Root `SKILL.md` is shipped CONTENT, not meta-docs** — it describes brand voice/visual rules that consumers of this d124- **Branch is ahead of `origin/main` by 1 commit** (`git status`). The single non-initial commit is `checkpoint: pre-yol
>esign system must follow. It is NOT a description of how to work on this repo. Don't treat it as authoritative for build>o` — likely a working snapshot, not a curated change. Don't treat that commit as the reference state.
>/test/structure questions; cite `README.md` for that. 
133- **`ui_kits/*/index.html` load React + Babel + lucide via unpkg CDN** (`ui_kits/marketing/index.html:7-9`). These are r
>eference layouts only — they do not gate publish, are not part of the build, and won't run offline. Do not introduce rea 
>l bundling here without scoping the change explicitly. 
134- **`preview/*.html` cards target a 700px design width** with custom responsive scaling rules in `preview/_card.css`. To
>uch targets are pinned to ≥36px height; scaled padding via `clamp()`. Read `preview/_card.css` header comment before cha 
>nging card layouts.

docs/project/architecture.md — file removed in iteration-3 (was 96 lines)
# Architecture

## High-level shape

A library — not a service, not an app. The published artifact (`dist/`) is a tree-shakeable ES module containing 49 React components, type declarations, and one concatenated stylesheet. Consumers do `import { Button } from "@ossrandom/design-system"` plus `import "@ossrandom/design-system/styles.css"` once at app root.

There are no servers, no background workers, no message queues, and no external API calls. The repo also ships **non-published** developer surfaces (`preview/`, `ui_kits/`) that exist only as reference material.

```
                    consumer app
                         │ imports
                         ▼
            ┌─────────────────────────┐
            │ dist/index.js (ESM)     │  ← built from src/index.tsx (re-export only)
            │ dist/index.d.ts         │
            │ dist/tokens.{js,d.ts}   │
            │ dist/styles.css         │  ← colors_and_type.css + src/styles.css
            └─────────────────────────┘
                         │
                         ▼
            ┌─────────────────────────┐
            │ src/components/*.tsx    │  one file per component family
            │ uses src/internal/cx.ts │  the only internal util
            │ uses src/components.d.ts│  for prop types
            │ uses src/tokens.ts      │  for token type unions
            └─────────────────────────┘
```

## Components (modules)

Each "component" here is a `src/components/*.tsx` family file. Twelve runtime modules + one types module + one entry module.

### `src/index.tsx` — Public entry

- **Lives in:** `src/index.tsx`
- **Responsibility:** re-export every runtime component and every type. Has no logic.
- **Talks to:** every file under `src/components/` and the two type modules.
- **Owns:** the public package surface — adding a component requires editing this file.

### `src/components.d.ts` — All prop interfaces (types-only)

- **Lives in:** `src/components.d.ts`
- **Responsibility:** declare every component's `*Props` interface in a single file. Imported by component implementations and re-exported via `export type * from "./components"` in `src/index.tsx`.
- **Convention:** every `*Props` extends `BaseProps` (`id`, `className`, `style`, `data-testid`); arrays are `readonly`; events use specific React event types (`MouseEvent<HTMLButtonElement>`, etc.) — NOT `any`.
- **Note:** intentionally NOT colocated with implementations. See `docs/project/conventions.md` "Don't refactor".

### `src/tokens.ts` — Token type unions

- **Lives in:** `src/tokens.ts`
- **Responsibility:** TypeScript string-literal unions (`BrandColor`, `SemanticColor`, `ThemeMode`, `Size`, `SpaceSize`, `Radius`, `Shadow`, `FontFamily`, `FontWeight`, `TypeScale`, `Density`, `Direction`, `Axis`, `Align`, `Justify`).
- **Pure types** — no runtime values. The JS values for these tokens live in `colors_and_type.css` as CSS variables.

### `src/internal/cx.ts` — The ONLY internal util

- **Lives in:** `src/internal/cx.ts`
- **Responsibility:** `cx(...args)` className composer + `uid()` helper. Used by every component file.
- **Why it matters:** the codebase deliberately keeps internals minimal — there is no utility-belt. Don't add new internals here without explicit need.

### `src/components/*.tsx` — Runtime component families

Each file owns a thematic group; component bodies are colocated. Selected highlights:

- `theme.tsx` — `ThemeProvider` stamps `data-theme` attribute on `document.documentElement`; allows accent override via `--accent` CSS var; exposes `useTheme()` hook with a graceful no-op fallback when no provider is present (reads `prefers-color-scheme`).
- `feedback.tsx` — contains the `toast` imperative API + `ToastRegion` host component. Other feedback primitives (Alert, Modal, Drawer, Progress, Skeleton, Spin, Tooltip) live in the same file.
- `layout.tsx` — `Grid` is built as `Object.assign(GridFn, { Col: GridCol })`, so consumers do `<Grid.Col>` (`src/components/layout.tsx`).
- `data-display.tsx` — `Table<T extends object>` is generic over row type; `RadioGroup<V>`, `Select<V>`, `Combobox<V>`, `Tabs<K>`, `Menu<K>` are similarly generic.

## Layering / dependency rules

There is one explicit layer rule, surfaced by directory shape rather than by lint:

- `src/components/*.tsx` may import from `src/internal/*`, `src/components.d.ts`, `src/tokens.ts`. They should not import from each other (verify by grepping `from "../components/"` inside `src/components/`). [inferred — not exhaustively verified]
- `src/index.tsx` is import-only; it must not declare runtime logic.
- `src/internal/*` must not import from `src/components/*` (would be a cycle).

No lint rule enforces these — the convention is maintained by reviewers.

## Cross-cutting concerns

- **Logging:** none. This is a library; logging is the consumer's concern.
- **Error handling:** components return `React.ReactElement` (or `| null` for conditionally-rendered Modal/Drawer). No throws on bad props — TypeScript catches at compile time. Where a prop is genuinely required for accessibility (e.g. `IconButton.aria-label`), it's marked required in `components.d.ts:55`.
- **Auth / authz:** N/A.
- **Observability:** N/A.
- **Config:** consumers pass props; ThemeProvider accepts `mode`, `accent`, `fontFamily` overrides; CSS vars on `:root` / `[data-theme="…"]` are the configuration surface.
- **i18n:** no built-in i18n layer. Strings are passed in via props (`title`, `description`, `aria-label`, etc.). [inferred — confirmed by `components.d.ts` showing string-typed labels with no message-key abstraction]
- **a11y:** components include `role="alert"`, `aria-modal="true"`, `aria-label` requirements. No automated a11y testing in CI today.

## Why it's shaped this way

Rationale where visible:

- **Single types file** — header of `src/components.d.ts` notes "All components extend a base `BaseProps`" and the convention that arrays are `readonly`. The single-file approach keeps the entire public type surface visible in one diff.
- **`.rcs-*` class names** — chosen to give the design system a distinct namespace so consumers' Tailwind/CSS-Modules/etc. don't accidentally collide. No ADR exists to confirm this; rationale `[inferred]` from naming.
- **Zero runtime deps** — `package.json` enforces this; only React/React-DOM as peer. Likely reasoning: keep the published bundle small and avoid version conflicts in consumer apps. No ADR.

If you need authoritative rationale beyond the above, the git history is too short to help (only 2 commits — see `git log --oneline`).

iteration-2 (previous)
iteration-3 (current)
f1# Build & Runf1# Build & Run
n2 n
3This is genuinely non-trivial despite the repo's small size: dual-registry publish with a tag/version-sync gate, an in-b
>uild CSS concatenation step, and CI that currently has soft-fails on lint and test. The full pipeline is documented belo 
>w. 
42
5## Prerequisites3## Prerequisites
64
n7- **Node:** `>=18.18` (`package.json:engines`). CI uses Node 20 (`.github/workflows/ci.yml:25`).n5- **Node:** `>=18.18` (`package.json:53`). CI uses `node-version: 20` (`.github/workflows/ci.yml:18`).
8- **pnpm:** 9.x — pinned via `package.json:packageManager = "pnpm@9.12.0"`. Use Corepack or install pnpm 9 globally; npm6- **pnpm:** `9.12.0` is the declared `packageManager`. CI uses pnpm 9 (`.github/workflows/ci.yml:14`).
>/yarn are not supported by the tooling. 
9- **System packages:** none. No native deps, no `protoc`, no codegen.7- **OS / native deps:** none. Pure JS toolchain.
10- **Docker:** not required.
118
12## First-time setup9## First-time setup
1310
n14From `.github/SETUP.md` and `package.json:scripts`:n11```bash
12pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
13```
1514
n16```bashn15Source: `.github/workflows/ci.yml:24`. Lockfile is `pnpm-lock.yaml` (committed).
17# 1. Install dependencies (frozen lockfile, like CI)
18pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
19 
20# 2. Verify the toolchain works
21pnpm typecheck   # tsc --noEmit
22pnpm build       # produces dist/
23```
2416
25## Local development loop17## Local development loop
2618
nn19There is **no watch mode and no dev server**. The library is a `tsc`-built package; the iteration loop is:
20 
27```bash21```bash
n28pnpm typecheck       # fastest signal — strict TSn22pnpm typecheck    # tsc --noEmit
29pnpm lint            # eslint src/**/*.{ts,tsx}  (no-op today; no eslint config)23pnpm build        # emits dist/ with .js, .d.ts, source/declaration maps, and dist/styles.css
30pnpm test            # vitest run (no-op today; no test files)
31pnpm build           # full build to dist/
32pnpm preview         # echoes "Open preview/responsive-check.html in a browser"
33```24```
3425
n35There is **no dev server, no HMR, no watch mode** declared in `package.json`. To iterate visually:n26For visual checks, open one of the 38 HTML cards in `preview/` directly in a browser, or open `preview/responsive-check.
 >html` (an iframe viewer with a device-dimension picker). `pnpm preview` (`package.json:41`) just `echo`s the hint — it d
 >oes not start a server.
3627
n371. Run `pnpm build` once.n28Note: the `preview/` cards reference `colors_and_type.css` and use **static HTML** with `.rcs-*` classes, not the live R
 >eact components. They test CSS, not component logic.
382. Open `preview/responsive-check.html` directly in a browser — it iframes the 39 `preview/components-*.html` cards acro
>ss simulated device viewports. 
393. For full layouts, open `ui_kits/marketing/index.html` (or `app/`, `docs/`) — these load React + Babel from unpkg at r
>untime and render in-browser. **Requires internet access** (CDN scripts). 
4029
41## Test layers30## Test layers
4231
n43- **Unit:** `pnpm test` runs vitest. **Zero test files exist** (verify: `find src -name '*.test.*' -o -name '*.spec.*'` n32- **Unit:** Vitest 2 declared (`package.json:53`). **Zero tests in the repo today** — verify via `find . -name '*.test.*
>→ empty).>' -not -path '*/node_modules/*'`. CI calls `pnpm run test` with `continue-on-error: true` (`.github/workflows/ci.yml:38`
 >).
44- **Integration:** none.33- **Integration / E2E:** none.
45- **E2E:** none.34- **Visual:** manual, via `preview/` HTML cards.
46- **Visual QA:** manual, via `preview/*.html` (39 design-gallery cards) and `ui_kits/*/index.html` (3 reference layouts)
>. Not automated. 
47- **a11y:** no automated check.
4835
n49If you're adding the first test:n36When you add the first test, plan to remove `continue-on-error: true` from the CI step in the same PR — otherwise broken
 > tests will silently pass green.
50- vitest is already in `devDependencies`. Default config (no `vitest.config.*` exists) — vitest will pick up `*.test.ts(
>x)` colocated with sources. 
51- Remove `continue-on-error: true` from the test step in `.github/workflows/ci.yml:40` once a real test exists, or the g
>reen CI status is meaningless. 
5237
53## Build artifacts38## Build artifacts
5439
n55Running `pnpm build` produces `dist/` with:n40`pnpm build` runs (`package.json:37`):
5641
n57- `dist/index.js` — ESM bundle (re-export only; one file per family is preserved as separate `.js` files because `tsc` mn42```bash
>irrors the `src/` tree) 
58- `dist/index.d.ts`, `dist/tokens.d.ts`, `dist/components.d.ts` — type declarations43tsc -p tsconfig.build.json && \
59- `dist/*.js.map`, `dist/*.d.ts.map` — source maps + declaration maps (`tsconfig.json:sourceMap: true, declarationMap: t44node -e "require('fs').writeFileSync(
>rue`) 
60- `dist/styles.css` — `colors_and_type.css` + `src/styles.css` concatenated by the inline Node script in `package.json:s45  'dist/styles.css',
>cripts.build` 
61 46  require('fs').readFileSync('colors_and_type.css','utf8') + '\n' +
62The `package.json:exports` map only routes consumers to:47  require('fs').readFileSync('src/styles.css','utf8')
63- `"."` → `dist/index.{js,d.ts}`48)"
64- `"./tokens"` → `dist/tokens.{js,d.ts}`
65- `"./styles.css"` → `dist/styles.css`
66 
67Other emitted files exist on disk but are not part of the public surface. [inferred — verified via `package.json:exports
>`] 
68 
69## CI
70 
71`.github/workflows/ci.yml` runs on push to `main` and PRs to `main`:
72 
73```
74checkout → setup pnpm@9 → setup Node 20 → pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
75   → typecheck   (hard fail)
76   → lint        (continue-on-error)
77   → test        (continue-on-error)
78   → build       (hard fail)
79   → upload dist artifact (push events only, retained 7 days)
80```49```
8150
n82Job timeout: 12 minutes. Concurrency group cancels in-progress runs on the same ref.n51What gets produced under `dist/`:
8352
n84**Important:** the lint and test steps are soft-fails (`continue-on-error: true`, lines 36 and 40). A green CI today mean53| File | Source |
>ns typecheck + build succeeded, NOT that tests or lint passed. Remove these flags once eslint config + tests land. 
54|------|--------|
55| `dist/index.js` + `dist/index.d.ts` | from `src/index.tsx` via `tsc` |
56| `dist/tokens.js` + `dist/tokens.d.ts` | from `src/tokens.ts` |
57| `dist/components.d.ts` | from `src/components.d.ts` (types-only) |
58| Per-component `.js` + `.d.ts` files | from `src/components/*.tsx` |
59| `dist/internal/cx.js` + `.d.ts` | from `src/internal/cx.ts` |
60| Source maps (`.js.map`) and declaration maps (`.d.ts.map`) | enabled in `tsconfig.json` |
61| `dist/styles.css` | concatenation of `colors_and_type.css` + `src/styles.css` |
8562
n86## How to releasen63`tsconfig.build.json` (verified, full file):
8764
n88Quick path (from `README.md > Releases`):n65```json
66{
67  "extends": "./tsconfig.json",
68  "compilerOptions": {
69    "noEmit": false,
70    "emitDeclarationOnly": false,
71    "outDir": "dist"
72  },
73  "include": ["src/**/*"],
74  "exclude": ["**/*.test.ts", "**/*.test.tsx", "node_modules", "dist", "preview", "src/styles.css"]
75}
76```
77 
78`package.json:32` declares `"files": ["dist", "src", "README.md"]` — so the published tarball includes the `src/` tree a
 >s well as `dist/`. (`.npmignore` exists at the repo root; verify excluded paths if needed.)
79 
80`package.json:31` declares `"sideEffects": ["**/*.css"]` so JS modules are tree-shakeable while CSS imports are preserve
 >d.
81 
82## Release pipeline
83 
84Triggered by tag push `v*.*.*` (or manual via Actions UI). Source: `.github/workflows/release.yml`.
85 
86Four jobs:
87 
881. **`build` — Build & verify.** `pnpm install`, `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm build`, then **refuse to continue if `package.j
 >son` version != tag** (the workflow does `[ "$TAG" != "v$VERSION" ] && exit 1`). Uploads `dist/`, `package.json`, `READM
 >E.md` as the `dist` artifact.
892. **`publish-npm`.** Downloads the `dist` artifact, sets up Node with `registry-url: https://registry.npmjs.org` and `s
 >cope: @ossrandom`, runs `npm publish --provenance --access public`. Authenticated via `NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_
 >TOKEN }}`. Wired to a GitHub `environment: npm` — set up an environment with required reviewer to gate publishes (`.gith
 >ub/SETUP.md` step 3).
903. **`publish-gpr`.** Same package, registry rewritten to `https://npm.pkg.github.com`, auth via auto-provided `GITHUB_T
 >OKEN`.
914. **GitHub Release.** Auto-generated notes + the `.tgz` tarball attached.
92 
93### Cutting a release
94 
95Source: `.github/RELEASING.md`.
8996
90```bash97```bash
n91pnpm version minor       # bumps package.json + creates v0.2.0 tagn98pnpm version patch    # 0.1.0 → 0.1.1 (or minor / major)
92git push --follow-tags99git push --follow-tags
93```100```
94101
n95What happens behind the scenes (`.github/workflows/release.yml`):n102`pnpm version` writes the new version into `package.json` and creates the matching tag atomically — that's why the relea
 >se workflow's tag-vs-version check passes when you use it. **Do not edit `package.json` version by hand**; the workflow 
 >will reject the tag.
96103
n971. **`build` job** — checkout the tag, install, `pnpm run typecheck && pnpm run build`, then **verify `git tag` (`vX.Y.Zn104### Required secrets
>`) matches `package.json:version`**. Hard-fails if mismatched. Uploads `dist/` + `package.json` + `README.md` as artifac 
>t. 
982. **`publish-npm` job** (env: `npm`) — downloads artifact, sets registry to `https://registry.npmjs.org`, runs `npm pub
>lish --provenance --access public`. Auth via `NPM_TOKEN` secret. 
993. **`publish-gpr` job** (env: `github-packages`) [inferred env name — actual env name not verified] — downloads artifac
>t, sets registry to `https://npm.pkg.github.com`, **mutates `package.json:publishConfig` in-place via inline Node** to p 
>oint at GPR, then `npm publish`. Auth via the auto-provided `GITHUB_TOKEN`. 
1004. **`github-release` job** — packs the artifact into `.tgz` via `npm pack`, then `softprops/action-gh-release@v2` creat
>es a Release with auto-generated notes and the `.tgz` attached. 
101105
n102Both publish jobs need the `build` job to succeed; the GitHub Release waits on both publishes.n106Source: `.github/SETUP.md` step 2.
103107
n104## Required secretsn108| Secret | Where used | Notes |
109|--------|-----------|-------|
110| `NPM_TOKEN` | `publish-npm` job | npmjs.com → Granular access token, "Publish for @ossrandom" |
111| `GITHUB_TOKEN` | `publish-gpr` job, `GitHub Release` step | Auto-provided by Actions, no setup needed |
105112
n106From `.github/SETUP.md:2`:n113## Deploy
107114
n108| Secret | Where it's used | How to provision |n115Not applicable — this is a published library. The "deploy" surface is the npm + GitHub Packages registries plus the GitH
 >ub Release page.
109|---|---|---|
110| `NPM_TOKEN` | `publish-npm` job | npmjs.com → Access Tokens → Granular → "Publish for @ossrandom" |
111| `GITHUB_TOKEN` | `publish-gpr` and `github-release` jobs | Auto-provided by GitHub Actions; no setup needed |
112 
113## Rollback
114 
115From `.github/SETUP.md > Rollback`. [Inferred — content not exhaustively read for this summary; consult the file directl
>y for the npm-deprecate / unpublish-window specifics before any rollback action.] 
116116
117## Gotchas117## Gotchas
118118
t119- **The build's CSS concat is a `node -e` one-liner** (`package.json:scripts.build`):t119- **CSS concat, not bundle.** `pnpm build` produces `dist/styles.css` via `Node fs.writeFileSync(... readFileSync('color
 >s_and_type.css') + '\n' + readFileSync('src/styles.css'))`. If you rename, move, or split either input CSS file, **the b
 >uild silently emits a broken stylesheet**. Update `package.json:37` in the same diff.
120  ```120- **`tsconfig.build.json` `exclude` lists `src/styles.css`** so TypeScript doesn't try to type-check the CSS file via th
 >e broad `include: ["src/**/*"]` glob. Don't remove the exclude when restructuring.
121  tsc -p tsconfig.build.json && node -e "require('fs').writeFileSync('dist/styles.css', require('fs').readFileSync('colo121- **CI is currently lenient.** Lint and test steps both use `continue-on-error: true` (`.github/workflows/ci.yml:34, :38
>rs_and_type.css','utf8') + '\n' + require('fs').readFileSync('src/styles.css','utf8'))">`) with `# remove once eslint config lands` / `# remove once tests land` comments. Removing those flags should be coordi
 >nated with landing the corresponding config / first test.
122  ```122- **Tag-version mismatch hard-fails the release.** `release.yml` does an explicit `if [ "$TAG" != "$PKG" ]; then exit 1;
 > fi`. Use `pnpm version <bump>` and `git push --follow-tags`; never edit `package.json` version separately from the tag.
123  Renaming either CSS file silently breaks `dist/styles.css`. If you split the concat into a real script, keep it equall123- **`environment: npm` may gate publishes.** If a required reviewer is configured (`.github/SETUP.md` step 3 recommends 
>y simple — no toolchain creep.>this), the npm publish step waits for manual approval. Pipeline jobs after `publish-npm` (GitHub Packages, GitHub Releas
 >e) won't run until approval lands.
124- **`tsconfig.build.json:exclude` lists `src/styles.css`** even though `tsc` would skip it anyway. The exclude is a load124- **`environment: github-packages`** likely exists for the `publish-gpr` job — `[inferred]`: not seen in the truncated p
>-bearing reminder that styles ship via the concat step.>ortion of `release.yml` indexed here. Verify by reading the bottom of `.github/workflows/release.yml` if you need to set
 > up reviewers there too.
125- **No `vitest.config.*` exists** — verify with `ls vitest* vite*` (returns nothing). vitest will use defaults; you'll n125- **Branch is ahead of `origin/main` by 1 unpushed commit** (`git status`, verified). The unpushed commit is `b0ea3b1 ch
>eed to add a config when introducing JSX-aware tests (e.g. for `jsdom`).>eckpoint: pre-yolo`. CI on `origin/main` reflects the prior `b5f630a` state until the checkpoint is pushed.
126- **No `.eslintrc*` / `eslint.config.*` exists** — verify with `ls .eslintrc* eslint.config.*`. The `lint` script will e126- **`pnpm preview` is a placeholder.** It does not start a server — it just `echo`s a hint. Open the HTML files directly
>ventually fail loudly when an eslint 9 flat-config file is added without `eslint` already being able to find it.> in a browser.
127- **Release publishes from the artifact, not from source** — `publish-npm` and `publish-gpr` jobs use `actions/download-127- **No Storybook, no test runner output, no visual diff.** Once you build, verify components by hand in `preview/`. Ther
>artifact@v4`, not `actions/checkout`. The `dist/`, `package.json`, and `README.md` shipped come from the `build` job's o>e is no automated UI gate.
>utput. 
128- **GPR job mutates `package.json` in the runner** before publishing — this rewrite is purely in the runner's filesystem
>; the repo's `package.json` is unchanged. Don't be alarmed by the inline Node script. 
129- **Tag format must be `vX.Y.Z` or `vX.Y.Z-…`** — release workflow trigger is `tags: - "v*.*.*"` (`.github/workflows/rel
>ease.yml`). A tag like `0.2.0` (no `v` prefix) won't trigger anything. 
130- **Don't try to publish manually with `npm publish`** — it would skip the tag/version-sync gate, the provenance flag, a
>nd the GPR mirror. Always go through the workflow. 
131- **`prepublishOnly` script** runs `pnpm run typecheck && pnpm run build`. This protects local `npm publish` invocations
> but doesn't substitute for the CI-side tag-vs-version check. 
132- **First-build oddities:** none observed — clean clone → `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` → `pnpm build` works in one p
>ass per the `ci.yml` flow. 
133- **Air-gapped builds:** `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` resolves from the lockfile but still hits the configured regis
>try on a clean install. For an air-gapped pipeline, mirror the registry (Verdaccio/Sonatype Nexus) and override via `.np 
>mrc`. 
134- **`ui_kits/*/index.html` runtime CDN deps** — these reference `unpkg.com` for React/React-DOM/Babel/lucide. They will 
>not run offline. They are not part of the build, not part of the published package, and not gated by CI. 
docs/project/conventions.md — file removed in iteration-3 (was 77 lines)
# Conventions

Rules to follow when modifying this code. Each item: the rule, an example file showing it, and the *why* if it's non-obvious.

## Code style

- **Strict TypeScript** — `tsconfig.json` sets `"strict": true`, `"noImplicitOverride": true`, `"noFallthroughCasesInSwitch": true`, `"isolatedModules": true`. Don't relax these.
- **JSX runtime:** `"jsx": "react-jsx"` (`tsconfig.json`) — no `import React` shim required for JSX; only import React explicitly when you need its API (`import * as React from "react"` is the prevailing form, see `src/components/theme.tsx:6`).
- **Component bodies:** the prevailing form is `export function Foo(props: FooProps): React.ReactElement { ... }`. Use `React.forwardRef<HTMLElement, FooProps>` only when the component needs to forward a DOM ref (current users: `Button`, `IconButton`, `Input`, `Textarea`).
- **`cx(...)` for className composition** — see `src/internal/cx.ts`. Don't introduce `clsx` or `classnames` — the in-house helper is the standard.

## Error handling

- **No throws.** This is a library; mis-typed props are a TypeScript error at compile time. Modal / Drawer return `null` when closed (`src/components/feedback.tsx`). If a prop is required for a11y, mark it required at the type level (e.g. `IconButton["aria-label"]: string` in `src/components.d.ts:55`).
- **Defensive SSR guards:** `if (typeof document === "undefined") return;` — see `src/components/theme.tsx:30`. Use this pattern in any effect that touches `document` / `window`.

## Naming

- **Files:** kebab-case for filenames in `src/components/` (`form-controls.tsx`, `data-display.tsx`).
- **CSS classes:** `.rcs-*` BEM-ish — block (`.rcs-button`), modifier (`.rcs-button--primary`, `.rcs-button--block`), element/slot (`.rcs-modal-header`, `.rcs-modal-body`). See full list in `src/styles.css`.
- **Type unions:** PascalCase singletons (`BrandColor`, `Size`, `ThemeMode`) in `src/tokens.ts`. Always string-literal unions, never plain `string`.
- **Component prop interfaces:** `<ComponentName>Props` in `src/components.d.ts`.

## Tests

- **Where:** there are no tests today. `package.json:scripts.test` is `vitest run`; `vitest` is in `devDependencies`. CI's test step has `continue-on-error: true` (`.github/workflows/ci.yml:40`).
- **Convention if you add one:** vitest, colocate as `*.test.ts` / `*.test.tsx` next to the file under test. The `tsconfig.build.json:exclude` already excludes `**/*.test.ts(x)` from the build, so tests won't ship to `dist/`. The `.npmignore` also excludes them.
- **CI gate:** when adding the first real test, also remove the `continue-on-error: true` line on the test step (and the lint step, once eslint config lands) — otherwise green CI continues to mean nothing.

## Logging

- N/A — this is a presentational library, no logging.

## Adding a new component

The high-leverage recipe (per `README.md > Editing components`):

> 1. Create `src/components/<family>.tsx` (or add to an existing family file).
> 2. Add the `<Component>Props` interface to `src/components.d.ts`. Extend `BaseProps`. Use strict event types (`(e: MouseEvent<HTMLButtonElement>) => void`, never `any`). Use `readonly` on array props.
> 3. Implement using `import * as React from "react"`, `import { cx } from "../internal/cx"`, and types from `../components` and `../tokens`.
> 4. Add `.rcs-<component>` styles to `src/styles.css` (BEM modifiers and slots).
> 5. Add a `export { Component } from "./components/<family>"` line to `src/index.tsx`.
> 6. (Optional) Add a `preview/components-<component>.html` design-gallery card to demo it.

## Adding a new design token

1. Add the CSS custom property to `colors_and_type.css` (likely under `:root, [data-theme="light"]` AND `[data-theme="dark"]`).
2. If it's a discrete-set token (e.g. a new `Size`), add the literal-union member in `src/tokens.ts`.
3. If it's a hex brand color exposed via `ThemeProvider.accent`, add it to the `BRAND_HEX` map in `src/components/theme.tsx`.

## Things to avoid (anti-patterns)

- **Don't add runtime dependencies.** `package.json` has zero `dependencies`. If you genuinely need a runtime dep, surface the trade-off explicitly (bundle size + version conflict risk for consumers) before adding.
- **Don't introduce a new styling system** (Tailwind, styled-components, css-in-js, CSS Modules). Hand-authored CSS with `.rcs-*` is the standard.
- **Don't add a bundler.** The current setup uses `tsc` directly + a tiny CSS-concat step. Adding Vite/Rollup/Webpack creates a new failure surface and changes the published output shape.
- **Don't import across families.** A component in `feedback.tsx` should not `import { Button } from "../components/buttons"` — keep families self-contained. (Reach for shared primitives via `src/internal/` instead — though today only `cx` and `uid` live there.) [inferred convention — verify by `grep '../components/' src/components/`]
- **Don't put runtime values in `src/tokens.ts`.** It's types-only; runtime values are CSS variables.

## Don't refactor (intentional non-standard choices)

These look unusual but are deliberate. Each entry: what it is, why it exists, and where to verify. **Do not "fix" these without explicit maintainer input.**

> **Single `src/components.d.ts` for ALL component prop types** — instead of colocating each `<Component>Props` next to its implementation. The header comment in `components.d.ts` lays out the conventions enforced by this layout (every `Props` extends `BaseProps`, strict events, readonly arrays). Rationale [inferred — no ADR]: keeps the entire public type surface visible in one diff and forces uniform conventions. Do not split.

> **One `src/components/*.tsx` file per family**, not per component — `feedback.tsx` ships 9 components together. Adds friction to grep-by-component but makes it trivial to share local helpers within a family. Do not split into per-component files.

> **CSS in a single hand-authored `src/styles.css`** with `.rcs-*` BEM classes, concatenated with `colors_and_type.css` at build time. The build script in `package.json:scripts.build` is a one-liner Node `-e` doing the concat. Do not migrate to css-in-js, CSS Modules, or per-component CSS.

> **Two CSS files at different roots:** `colors_and_type.css` lives at repo root (NOT inside `src/`); `src/styles.css` lives inside `src/`. The build concatenates them in that order. Rationale [inferred]: the root file is the "drop-in token sheet" that consumers can also use standalone (it's referenced from `ui_kits/*/index.html` and `preview/*.html`). Don't move either.

> **`tsc`, no bundler.** ESM-only output, no Vite/Rollup. Verify: `package.json` has no bundler in `devDependencies`. Don't introduce one.

> **Zero runtime dependencies.** `package.json:dependencies` is absent — only `peerDependencies` and `devDependencies` are listed. This is a hard line.

> **`SKILL.md` at the repo root is content the project ships, not docs about the project.** It targets *consumers of the design system* (brand voice / visual rules they should follow when building with the system). Don't refactor it into a contributor-facing doc and don't conflate its rules with this repo's contributor conventions.

> **`ui_kits/*/index.html` load React via unpkg CDN.** They are reference layouts only, not part of the published package and not exercised in CI. Do not bundle them, do not enforce CI on them, and do not assume they're production code.

iteration-3 (current)

>e, fetch, or own forms within. Sections that don't apply to libraries are omitted.>tching / Forms-as-major-surface are intentionally omitted (they describe an app, not a library).

n7- **Framework:** React 18+ (peer dep, `package.json:peerDependencies`).n7- **Framework:** React 18+ (peer dep — `package.json:46–49`).
8- **Build tool:** TypeScript compiler (`tsc -p tsconfig.build.json`) + a Node one-liner that concatenates two CSS files 8- **Build tool:** plain `tsc` to JS + `.d.ts`. No bundler. CSS produced by a Node `fs` one-liner concatenating `colors_a
>into `dist/styles.css`. No bundler (Vite/Rollup/Webpack) — TypeScript emits ESM directly.>nd_type.css` + `src/styles.css` — see `package.json:37`.
9- **Styling:** Hand-authored CSS in `src/styles.css`, BEM-ish `.rcs-*` class names. Tokens as CSS custom properties in `9- **Styling:** hand-rolled BEM with the `.rcs-*` prefix. 251 unique classes in `src/styles.css` (verify: `grep -oE '\.rc
>colors_and_type.css` at the repo root.>s-[a-z0-9-]+' src/styles.css | sort -u | wc -l`). No CSS-in-JS, no CSS Modules, no Tailwind, no PostCSS.
10- **State management:** local component state only (`React.useState`). One `React.Context` exists: `ThemeContext` in `sr10- **State management:** local React state per component (`useState` / `useEffect`). The `toast` API is the one shared st
>c/components/theme.tsx`.>ore — an in-memory queue rendered by `<ToastRegion />` (`src/components/feedback.tsx:230` and `:265`).

n14Re-exported from `src/index.tsx`. 49 components, grouped by family. Source of truth for props is `src/components.d.ts`.n14- **Public entry:** `src/index.tsx` (35 lines). Pure re-export module — `export type * from "./tokens"`, `export type * 
 >from "./components"`, then named runtime re-exports per component file.
15 15- **No app shell.** This is a library; consumers wrap their own tree in `<ThemeProvider>` and optionally render `<ToastR
 >egion />` once near the root (`README.md` § "Mounting the toast region").
16| Family | Components | File |16- **Provider stack consumers should use:** `<ThemeProvider mode="light|dark" accent? fontFamily?>` is the only provider 
 >exported. There is no `QueryClientProvider`, `I18nProvider`, etc. — none shipped, none required.

>ield` | `src/components/form-controls.tsx` |

>ents/feedback.tsx` |

>ional and pre-1.0 — do not split.>`:
37- **All prop types in one file:** `src/components.d.ts` (~3.6KB × 7 sections). Top-of-file comment lists conventions: ev21 
>ery `*Props` extends `BaseProps`; events typed strictly; readonly arrays. 
38- **No story files, no Storybook.** Verify: `ls .storybook` returns nothing; no `*.stories.*` files. The "preview" surfa22| File | Components |
>ce is `preview/*.html` (39 standalone HTML cards).

> are plain function components. (Verify: `grep -l 'forwardRef' src/components/*.tsx`.)

38Each component file exports both `forwardRef`-wrapped functional components (e.g. `Button` in `buttons.tsx:10`) and plai
 >n functions (`PageHeader`, `AppShell`, etc.). The repo mixes the two depending on whether ref-forwarding is required for
 > the underlying element.

40**Single internal helper:** `src/internal/cx.ts` (verified — 23 lines). Exports `cx()` (truthy-string class joiner), `no
 >op()`, and `uid(prefix = "rcs")`. Components import `cx` from here; this is the only internal-shared module.
